channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: ⚛️ Navy Still Pushing To Field New AARGM-ER Radar-Busting Missile This Year Despite “Strategic Pause” | The War Zone
item.description: The Navy expects to see the AGM-88G AARGM-ER enter service in September despite not planning to buy any of them in the next fiscal year. The post Navy Still Pushing To Field New AARGM-ER Radar-Busting Missile This Year Despite “Strategic Pause” appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Australia boosts army with HIMARS and more armored vehicles | Breaking Defense
item.description: The HIMARS buy will come to $2.3 billion AUD, as the Australian military aims to “significantly enhance” its long-range strike capability.

item.title: ⚛️ India races to boost conventional, nuclear submarine combat punch | Defense News
item.description: The induction of a third SSBN brings the number of such boats to three, enabling the deployment of a continuous at-sea nuclear counterstrike force.

item.title: 🏛️ UK lawmaker report warns AUKUS under threat from ‘shortcomings and failings’ | Breaking Defense
item.description: Findings also claim that the Royal Navy’s Astute-class nuclear-powered submarines have been “stretched” to, or “even beyond,” their limits.

item.title: 📍 Accelerating space power through software-defined mission dominance | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] As space threats continue to evolve, existing satellites need new capabilities to defend and protect, even after launch.

item.title: 📍 The 2026 Global Energy Agenda | Atlantic Council
item.description: The Atlantic Council is pleased to present its sixth Global Energy Agenda that shares new insights from thought leaders and our annual survey of experts from around the world. The post The 2026 Global Energy Agenda appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: ⚛️ Nuclear energy for an era of rising demand and strategic risk | Atlantic Council
item.description: As electricity demand and geopolitical tensions rise, nuclear energy stands out as a crucial pillar in countries' energy security strategies. The post Nuclear energy for an era of rising demand and strategic risk appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 The infrastructure that will drive energy innovation and competitiveness | Atlantic Council
item.description: Companies across industries are investing in lower-carbon production. What's needed now to drive competition and innovation is the right carbon measurement system. The post The infrastructure that will drive energy innovation and competitiveness appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🏛️ Army releases commercial solutions opening for rapid EW and signals intelligence capabilities | Breaking Defense
item.description: The commercial solutions offering aims to produce a “library” that will provide options for units to determine what capabilities meet their structure and mission.

item.title: 📍 Golden Dome has ‘pathways to pivot’ if delays arise, general says | Defense News
item.description: Gen. Michael Guetlein, the appointed czar for America's “Golden Dome” initiative, told lawmakers that plans to build the shield were proceeding apace.

item.title: 📍 To Counter Rising Political Violence, America Needs to Reinforce Its Early Warning Infrastructure | Just Security
item.description: Stakeholders must reinforce U.S. monitoring and response infrastructure to address growing risks of political violence. The post To Counter Rising Political Violence, America Needs to Reinforce Its Early Warning Infrastructure appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 Forterra, Polaris tout new autonomous vehicle for CASEVAC, logistics | Breaking Defense
item.description: The MESA vehicle is designed for both logistics and casualty evacuation operations up to the “last tactical mile.”

item.title: 🏴 Russia’s War Boom Masks an Economic Implosion | Foreign Policy
item.description: Record-low unemployment is the result of millions of missing workers.

item.title: 🏛️ Textron unveils autonomous ground vehicle designed for Marine Corps littoral units | Defense One
item.description: The RIPSAW M1 demonstrator made its debut on the show floor at the Modern Day Marine conference.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: April 28, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE President Trump yesterday told advisers that he is not satisfied with Iran’s latest proposal to reopen…

item.title: 🌍 Iran Is More Unified Than Ever | Foreign Policy
item.description: The war has deepened Iranian officials’ connections with one another—and with the public.

item.title: 📍 The Mountaintop Mirage: Why Xi’s Military Purges Cannot Produce the Force He Wants | War on the Rocks
item.description: During the 1979 Sino-Vietnamese War, a 26-year-old company commander’s unit was pinned down by a fortified hilltop. After frontal assaults failed, the junior officer made an extraordinary request: an entire battalion, four times the size of his own unit, for a jungle flanking…

item.title: 🏴 The Strange Rise and Fall of Russia’s Crowd Sourced Defense Industry | War on the Rocks
item.description: In the early days of the full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022, much that could go wrong did for the Russian military. As one volunteer organization called KatyaValya recalled:We called all our military friends in (Russian-held) Donetsk, but no one could really explain…

item.title: 🌏 Expanding frontiers: China’s military push beyond the first island chain | ASPI Strategist
item.description: China’s defence and security presence beyond the Western Pacific is set to intensify over the next decade, expanding its access, influence and operational reach across the Indo-Pacific. In the Southwest Pacific, Beijing is likely to ...

item.title: 📍 Latin America’s Anti-Women Movement Is Spreading | Foreign Policy
item.description: Chile's president José Antonio Kast is following the regressive examples set elsewhere in the region.

item.title: 🏛️ What Congress Could Do to Stop the War | Foreign Policy
item.description: Republicans are declining to use their power of the purse.

item.title: 🌍 The Iran War Is Tearing Trump’s Coalition Apart | Foreign Policy
item.description: MAGA is not necessarily the same thing as America First.

item.title: 📍 The Disposable Oligarchs | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Why wealthy elites come to regret their bargains with authoritarians.

item.title: 🌍 Let Iran Defeat Itself | Foreign Affairs
item.description: America should end the war but keep up the pressure.

item.title: 📍 Myanmar Is Not ‘in Transition’: War by Other Means and the Risks of Policy Drift | The Diplomat
item.description: Rather than focusing on elite-level political maneuvering, foreign governments need to recognize the transformative potential of the country's resistance movement.

item.title: 📍 Former Malaysian PM Najib Withdraws Appeal Against House Arrest Ruling: Report | The Diplomat
item.description: The decision is a setback for the ex-leader, who was recently sentenced to an additional 15 years in prison for his central involvement in the 1MDB corruption scandal.

item.title: 📍 Why aircraft carriers are the best (and worst) place for laser weapons | Defense News
item.description: High-energy laser weapons are a natural fit for large, power-rich aircraft carriers — with limits.

item.title: 🏛️ What we know about the US military’s new joint laser weapon system | Defense News
item.description: The Army-Navy effort aims to produce a containerized 150-kilowatt high-energy laser weapon to counter incoming cruise missiles.

item.title: 🏛️ A concern for Australia: Britain looks unserious about naval defence | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australia should have a big concern. Britain, a country that Australia is relying on for its future submarine force and much else under AUKUS, looks seriously unserious about naval defence. Quite apart from the challenge ...

item.title: 🏴 EU Extends Sanctions Against Myanmar, Citing ‘Continuing Grave Situation’ | The Diplomat
item.description: The announcement comes as the country's new quasi-civilian government pursues diplomatic normalization with the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN).
